mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the accommodative zone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of June 2019 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 2.5% are not significantly higher than when FEDFUNDS >= 2.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment of more than 50,000 persons signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50,000 persons in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136,877 thousand as of June 2019
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not occur in > 65% of instances where PAYEMS declines > 50,000 in a month, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to trigger the signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 3% month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 431,879 million and HOUST at 1,091 thousand as of May 2019
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 3% simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism activates when production growth turns negative relative to recent trend.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average by > 0.5 index points
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 3-month MA by > 0.5 points, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that raise recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns do not occur in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in a month, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to accelerate simultaneously to activate the signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15,839.3 billion (Q1 2019) and HOUST at 1,091 thousand (May 2019)
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong demand momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 > 2.5% QoQ and HOUST > 5% MoM, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rates above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production combined with rising consumer prices signals stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and raises equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism activates when supply constraints meet demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.28 and CPIAUCSL at 233.89 as of May 2019
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where INDPRO declines > 1.0% MoM and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
